tools.name: ruff
tools.run_command: uv run ruff check ci tests
tools.fix_command: uv run ruff check --fix ci tests

tools.name: ruff-format
tools.run_command: uv run ruff format --check ci tests
tools.fix_command: uv run ruff format ci tests

tools.name: pyright
tools.scope: ci/ and tests/ directories (configured in pyproject.toml)

tools.name: pre-commit
tools.scope: Runs uv-lock, ruff, ruff-format, and pyright on configured paths

linting.notes: Pre-commit orchestrates all linters. Ruff config in pyproject.toml enables extensive rule sets (B, C4, COM, E, W, F, FA, FLY, G, I, INP, INT, ISC, N, PERF, PGH, PIE, PL, etc). Line length is 120. Pyright has typeCheckingMode=off but checks for missing imports and unbound variables.

env_vars: DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/$UID/podman/podman.sock (for container tests on Linux with podman)
testing.mock_patterns: Uses pyfakefs for filesystem mocking, testcontainers for container tests
testing.notes: Tests are categorized with pytest markers: @pytest.mark.openshift (requires OpenShift cluster), @pytest.mark.cuda (requires CUDA/GPU), @pytest.mark.rocm (requires ROCm/AMD GPU). Basic tests in tests/test_main.py and tests/pytest_tutorial/ run without infrastructure. Container tests in tests/containers/ require Docker/Podman and an --image parameter. One test (test_make_disable_pushing) requires make and a container engine.

ci.notes: The code-quality.yaml workflow runs on push, pull_request, and workflow_dispatch. All three jobs (check-generated-code, pytest-tests, code-static-analysis) are gating. Tekton pipelines build notebook container images for various Python versions, CUDA variants, ROCm variants, etc. Build workflows trigger on PR and push events.

build.system: uv (Python package manager) + Makefile (for container images)
build.notes: Project uses uv for Python dependency management (replaces pip/pipenv). uv.lock pins all dependencies. Makefile builds container images using podman/docker. The project is package-mode=false in pyproject.toml, meaning it's not installable as a Python package - it's a collection of notebook images.

conventions.import_style: from __future__ import annotations at top, absolute imports preferred, grouped and sorted by ruff/isort rules
conventions.notes: Code follows strict formatting (ruff format, line length 120). Tests use pytest markers for infrastructure requirements. Pipfile validation ensures each notebook variant declares correct Python version.

lint_commands.command: uv run ruff check ci tests
lint_commands.notes: Exit 0, 1 warning about invalid noqa comment (non-critical)

lint_commands.command: uv run ruff format --check ci tests

lint_commands.notes: Exit 0, all checks passed (uv-lock, ruff, ruff-format, pyright)

test_commands.notes: Exit 1, 2 passed, 1 failed (test_make_disable_pushing requires make + podman/docker)
Not fully validated - many tests require --image parameter, Docker/Podman, or infrastructure (OpenShift, GPU)

container_recipe.notes: The container validation demonstrates that basic lint and test commands work correctly. Container tests in tests/containers/ require a running Docker/Podman daemon and an --image parameter. Tests marked with @pytest.mark.openshift, @pytest.mark.cuda, or @pytest.mark.rocm require corresponding infrastructure. Basic validation tests (Pipfile checks, file consistency, tutorial tests) run successfully without external dependencies.

validation.summary: Install OK (94 packages), lint OK (ruff + pyright + pre-commit all passed), basic tests OK (6 passed). One test requires make+podman/docker. Container tests not validated (require infrastructure).

gaps: Container tests in tests/containers/ require Docker or Podman daemon and an --image parameter - not validated in basic container setup
Tests marked @pytest.mark.openshift require an OpenShift cluster for execution
Tests marked @pytest.mark.cuda require CUDA-capable GPU hardware
Tests marked @pytest.mark.rocm require ROCm-capable AMD GPU hardware
One test (test_make_disable_pushing) requires both make and a container engine, fails without them
Browser tests in tests/browser/ require Playwright setup - not validated
Full CI workflow includes yamllint, JSON validation, hadolint, and kustomize checks - these require additional tools not installed in basic Python container
Makefile-based image building requires podman/docker and significant build infrastructure
